title: CAM3MFExportOptions Object
description: 3MF export option. Available with all additive machines except Formlabs. Expects a setup as its export object.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: areSimulationSurrogatesSplit
properties.description: Flag toggling if surrogate supports used in the simulation should be split. This option might not be available for all machine types. The default value is false.

properties.name: areSimulationThickeningStructuresKept
properties.description: Flag toggling if thickening structures used for simulation should be kept. This option might not be available for all machine types. The default value is false.

properties.name: areSupportsIncluded
properties.description: **RETIRED** Flag toggling if support information should be included in the exported file. Includes both support structures marked as open or closed support as well as meta data used in Netfabb. This option might not be available for all machine types. The default value is false.

properties.name: error
properties.description: Gets the last encountered error message. When the CAMExportManager's executeWithExportFuture() method is used, this method only returns errors encoutered when setting up the export. Errors thrown afterwards can be retrieved via the CAMExportFuture object. When the CAMExportManager's execute() method is used, any error can be retrieved using this property.

properties.name: exportObject
properties.description: The export object we want to export. Depending on the actual export option, this might be geometry, an operation or a setup.

properties.name: fullFilename
properties.description: The file we want to export to. Needs to contain a valid path, as no intermediate folders are created.

properties.name: isMachineInformationIncluded
properties.description: Flag toggling if machine information should be included in the exported file. The machine information is only compatible with Netfabb. This option might not be available for all machine types. The default value is false.

properties.name: isProcessSimulationDataIncluded
properties.description: Flag toggling if simulation information should be included in the exported file. This option might not be available for all machine types. The default value is false.

properties.name: isSimulationPostProcessingIncluded
properties.description: Flag toggling if post processing of the simulation should be included. This option might not be available for all machine types. The default value is false.

properties.name: isSliceDataIncluded
properties.description: Flag toggling if slice data which has been generated beforehand by generating the entire setup or the additive toolpath object should be included in the exported file. The default value is false.

properties.name: isThumbnailSupported
properties.description: Method to check if the exporter implementation supports thumbnail

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: isVolumetricDataIncluded
properties.description: Flag toggling if volumetric data should be included in the exported file. The flag is only evaluated if the user has bought the product design extension. The default value is false.

properties.name: metadata
properties.description: Class for setting the meta data options with in the 3mf export

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: supportInclusion
properties.description: Flag setting if support information should be included in the exported file. Includes both support structures marked as open or closed support as well as meta data used in Netfabb. This option might not be available for all machine types. The default value is NotIncluded.

properties.name: thumbnailPath
properties.description: Path to the thumbnail for the buildfile

properties.name: volumetricDataResolution
properties.description: Integer value representing the resolution of the volumetric data. The value is only evaluated if the user has bought the product design extension. The default value is 128.

areSimulationSurrogatesSplit.title: CAM3MFExportOptions.areSimulationSurrogatesSplit Property
areSimulationSurrogatesSplit.description: Flag toggling if surrogate supports used in the simulation should be split. This option might not be available for all machine types. The default value is false.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = cAM3MFExportOptions_var.areSimulationSurrogatesSplit

# Set the value of the property.
cAM3MFExportOptions_var.areSimulationSurrogatesSplit = propertyValue

areSupportsIncluded.title: CAM3MFExportOptions.areSupportsIncluded Property
areSupportsIncluded.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Flag toggling if support information should be included in the exported file. Includes both support structures marked as open or closed support as well as meta data used in Netfabb. This option might not be available for all machine types. The default value is false. Remarks

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = cAM3MFExportOptions_var.areSupportsIncluded

# Set the value of the property.
cAM3MFExportOptions_var.areSupportsIncluded = propertyValue

classType.title: CAM3MFExportOptions.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.
